reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is 10.0% and 20-day momentum is 17.6%.
Trend quality is supported by 70% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 8.5% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 2.7%, downside volatility near 2.1%, and a recent max drawdown of 6.9%.
Volume confirmation is -20.2% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is 8.3% versus SPY over 5 days and 10.0% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is bullish with score 0.81, confidence 0.75, and 0.3 weighted articles across 2 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow bullish at 0.81, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 2 articles across 2 sources, 0.3 effective weighted articles, average relevance 0.71, average source quality 0.78, confidence 0.75.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC -0.02).
Overall model score is 0.229 with medium confidence.

news_evidence.title: Waymo Recalls Robotaxis After Vehicle Drove on a Flooded Road
news_evidence.llm_reason: Waymo recall is sector/autonomy-related news. Could modestly benefit Tesla’s relative perception on AV safety/competitiveness, but it’s indirect and unlikely to produce a large, sustained price move for TSLA within 1–10 trading days.

news_evidence.title: Understanding Tesla's Position In Automobiles Industry Compared To Competitors - Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA)
news_evidence.llm_reason: Benzinga piece is a comparative/analytical write-up about Tesla’s position and metrics. Informative for longer-term views but not new, material company-specific news — limited likelihood of moving TSLA over the next 1–10 days beyond marginal sentiment effects.
